children.text: in accordance with this section; and

for the purpose of providing nutrition assistance through electronic benefit transfer or methods described in clauses (ii) and (iii) of subsection (b)(2)(B) during the summer months for each eligible child, to ensure continued access to food when school is not in session for the summer.

children.heading: Benefits issued by states
children.label: A Benefits issued by states
children.text: Summer EBT benefits issued pursuant to subsection (a) by a State may only be used by the eligible household that receives such summer EBT benefits to purchase food (as defined in section 3 of the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 ()) from retail food stores that have been approved for participation in the supplemental nutrition assistance program established under such Act [ et seq.] and in accordance with section 7(b) of such Act () or in the nutrition assistance program in American Samoa, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.7 U.S.C. 20127 U.S.C. 20117 U.S.C. 2016(b)

children.heading: Benefits issued by covered Indian Tribal organizations
children.label: B Benefits issued by covered Indian Tribal organizations
children.text: Summer EBT benefits issued pursuant to subsection (a) by a covered Indian Tribal organization may only be used by the eligible household that receives such summer EBT benefits to purchase supplemental foods from vendors that have been approved for participation in the special supplemental nutrition program for women, infants, and children under .section 1786 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: for calendar year 2024, in an amount equal to $40, which may be proportionately higher consistent with the adjustments established under for each eligible child in the eligible household per month during the summer operational period; andsection 1760(f) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: for calendar year 2025 and each year thereafter, in an amount equal to the unrounded benefit amount from the prior year, adjusted to the nearest lower dollar increment to reflect changes to the cost of the diet described in section 3(u) of the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 () for the 12-month period ending on November 30 of the preceding calendar year and rounded to the nearest lower dollar increment; and7 U.S.C. 2012(u)

children.label: i
children.text: in the form of an EBT card;

children.label: ii
children.text: through other electronic methods, as determined by the Secretary; or

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of a State that does not issue nutrition assistance program benefits electronically, using the same methods by which that State issues benefits under the nutrition assistance program of that State.

children.label: A
children.text: be subject to sections 12, 14, and 15 of the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 (, 2023, 2024) and subsections (n), (), and (p) of , as applicable; and7 U.S.C. 2021section 1786 of this titleo

children.label: B
children.text: to the maximum extent practicable, incorporate technology tools consistent with industry standards that track or prevent theft of benefits, cloning, or other fraudulent activities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), summer EBT benefits issued pursuant to subsection (a) may only be issued for the purpose of purchasing food during the summer months, with appropriate issuance and expungement timelines as determined by the Secretary (but with an expungement timeline not to exceed 4 months).

children.heading: Continuous school calendar
children.label: B Continuous school calendar
children.text: In the case of children who are under a continuous school calendar, the Secretary shall establish alternative plans for the period during which summer EBT benefits may be issued pursuant to subsection (a) and used.

children.label: A
children.text: with respect to summer, automatically enroll each eligible child who is directly certified, is an identified student (as defined in ), or is otherwise determined by a school food authority to be eligible to receive free or reduced price meals in the instructional year immediately preceding the summer or during the summer operational period in the program under this section, without further application from households;section 1759a(a)(1)(F)(i) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: make an application available for children who do not meet the criteria described in subparagraph (A) and make eligibility determinations using the eligibility criteria for free or reduced price lunches under this chapter;

children.label: C
children.text: establish procedures to carry out the enrollment described in subparagraph (A);

children.label: D
children.text: establish procedures for expunging summer EBT benefits from the account of a household, consistent with the requirements under subsection (b)(4); and

children.label: E
children.text: allow eligible households to opt out of participation in the program under this section and establish procedures for opting out of such participation.

children.heading: Covered Indian Tribal organization requirements
children.label: 2 Covered Indian Tribal organization requirements
children.text: Covered Indian Tribal organizations participating in the program under this section shall, to the maximum extent practicable, meet the requirements under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Administrative expenses
children.label: d Administrative expenses
children.text: The Secretary shall pay to each State agency and covered Indian Tribal organization an amount equal to 50 percent of the administrative expenses incurred by the State agency or covered Indian Tribal organization in operating the program under this section, including the administrative expenses of local educational agencies and other agencies in each State or covered Indian Tribal organization relating to the operation of the program under this section.

children.heading: Summer EBT authority
children.label: e Summer EBT authority
children.text: Beginning in summer 2024, the Secretary shall not allow States to use the authority in section 749(g) of the Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2010 (; ), to provide access to food through electronic benefit transfer benefits to children during the summer months when schools are not in regular session.Public Law 111–80123 Stat. 2132

children.heading: Issuance of interim final regulations
children.label: f Issuance of interim final regulations
A
children.text: section 749(g) of the Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2010 (; ); andPublic Law 111–80123 Stat. 2132

children.label: B
children.text: the pandemic EBT program under section 1101 of the Families First Coronavirus Response Act ( note; );7 U.S.C. 2011Public Law 116–127
ensure timely and fair service to applicants for and recipients of benefits under this section;

children.label: A
children.text: to correctly determine the eligibility of children for benefits under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: to reliably enroll and issue benefits to eligible children; and

children.label: A
children.text: automatically enrolling each eligible child who is able to be directly certified; and

children.label: B
children.text: establishing a single summer operational period.

children.heading: Administrative and management plan
children.label: g Administrative and management plan
children.text: Beginning in 2024, each State desiring to participate in the program under this section shall comply with the requirements under .section 1761(n) of this title

children.heading: Covered Indian Tribal organization
children.label: 1 Covered Indian Tribal organization
children.text: The term “covered Indian Tribal organization” means an Indian Tribal organization that participates in the special supplemental nutrition program for women, infants, and children established under .section 1786 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: certified to receive free or reduced price lunch under the school lunch program under this chapter;

children.label: ii
children.text: certified to receive free or reduced price breakfast under the school breakfast program under ; orsection 1773 of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: able to be directly certified;

children.label: i
children.text: enrolled in a school described in subparagraph (B), (C), (D), (E), or (F) of ; andsection 1759a(a)(1) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: an identified student (as defined in ); orsection 1759a(a)(1)(F)(i) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: a child who otherwise met the requirements to receive free or reduced price meals, as determined through an application process using the eligibility criteria for free or reduced price meals under this chapter; or

children.label: C
children.text: has been determined to be eligible for the program under this section in accordance with subsection (c)(1)(B).

children.heading: Eligible household
children.label: 3 Eligible household
children.text: The term “eligible household” means a household that includes at least 1 eligible child.

children.label: i
children.text: containing nutrients determined by nutritional research to be lacking in the diets of children; and

children.label: ii
children.text: that promote the health of the population served by the program under this section, as indicated by relevant nutrition science, public health concerns, and cultural eating patterns, as determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: i
children.text: provide the nutritional equivalent of foods described in such subparagraph; and

children.label: ii
children.text: allow for different cultural eating patterns than foods described in such subparagraph.
